data.text: I’m reading the agent runner and service-run paths first to verify the bead scope against the acceptance criteria, then I’ll patch only the minimal code needed and run the targeted Go tests plus pre-commit.

I’ve confirmed the service runner still has the event-drain logic in one place. Next I’m narrowing to the exact event types, result projection, and tests so I can remove the stringly-typed handling without widening scope.

    40	// appendProviderTimeoutHint appends a configuration override hint to errMsg
    41	// when it indicates a provider request timeout fired. Helps operators find
    42	// the knob to adjust (AC4 of ddx-2c63bb95).
    43	func appendProviderTimeoutHint(errMsg string, providerTimeout time.Duration) string {
    44		if errMsg == "" || !strings.Contains(errMsg, "provider request timeout") {
    45			return errMsg
    46		}
    47		return errMsg + fmt.Sprintf(
    48			"; request_timeout=%s exceeded — override via"+
    49				" agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml"+
    50				" or pass --request-timeout DURATION to execute-bead/execute-loop",
    51			providerTimeout.Round(time.Second),
    52		)
    53	}
    54	
    55	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
    56	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
    57	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
    58	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
    59	//
    60	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
    61	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
    62	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
    63	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
    64	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
    65	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
    66	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
    67	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
    68		if ctx == nil {
    69			ctx = context.Background()
    70		}
    71	
    72		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
    73	
    74		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
    75			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
    76			if sessionLogDir == "" {
    77				sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
    78			}
    79			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")}
    80			r := NewRunner(cfg)
    81			r.WorkDir = workDir
    82			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{
    83				Context:       ctx,
    84				Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
    85				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,
    86				PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
    87				PromptSource:  runtime.PromptSource,
    88				Correlation:   runtime.Correlation,
    89				Model:         rcfg.Model(),
    90				Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
    91				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
    92				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
    93				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
    94				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
    95				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
    96				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
    97				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
    98			})
    99		}
   100	
   101		factory := serviceRunFactory
   102		if factory == nil {
   103			factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
   104		}
   105		svc, err := factory(workDir)
   106		if err != nil {
   107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
   108		}
   109		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, workDir, rcfg, runtime)
   110	}

data.text: I’ve confirmed production already uses `DecodeServiceEvent` in the drain path. The remaining risk is test coverage and any leftover stringly-typed event construction, so I’m reading those tests now and will tighten them to the opaque-forwarding contract.

1	package agent

     3	import (

     5		"testing"

     8		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
     9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

    12	
    13	// TestDrainServiceEvents_CapturesRouteEconomics covers the routing_decision
    14	// event's winning candidate (eligible=true, model matches payload.model)
    15	// carrying power, speed, and cost telemetry. DDx must surface those fields
    16	// from the selected eligible candidate without touching the final event.
    17	func TestDrainServiceEvents_CapturesRouteEconomics(t *testing.T) {
    18		events := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 4)
    19	
    20		routingPayload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
    21			"harness":  "agent",
    22			"provider": "anthropic",
    23			"model":    "claude-3-5-sonnet",
    24			"candidates": []map[string]any{
    25				{
    26					"model":      "claude-3-haiku",
    27					"eligible":   false,
    28					"components": map[string]any{"power": 20},
    29				},
    30				{
    31					"model":                  "claude-3-5-sonnet",
    32					"eligible":               true,
    33					"cost_usd_per_1k_tokens": 0.0125,
    34					"cost_source":            "catalog",
    35					"components": map[string]any{
    36						"power":     65,
    37						"speed_tps": 42.5,
    38					},
    39				},
    40			},
    41		})
    42		require.NoError(t, err)
    43		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
    44			Type: "routing_decision",
    45			Time: time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
    46			Data: routingPayload,
    47		}
    48	
    49		finalPayload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
    50			"status":     "success",
    51			"exit_code":  0,
    52			"final_text": "done",
    53		})
    54		require.NoError(t, err)
    55		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
    56			Type: "final",
    57			Time: time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 0, 1, 0, time.UTC),
    58			Data: finalPayload,
    59		}
    60		close(events)
    61	
    62		_, routing, _ := drainServiceEvents(events)
    63		require.NotNil(t, routing)
    64		power, speed, cost, source := selectedRoutingCandidateMetrics(routing)
    65		assert.Equal(t, 65, power,
    66			"power must come from the eligible winning candidate in routing_decision.candidates")
    67		assert.Equal(t, 42.5, speed)
    68		assert.Equal(t, 0.0125, cost)
    69		assert.Equal(t, "catalog", source)
    70	}
    71	
    72	// TestDrainServiceEvents_ForwardsCanonicalProgressPayload proves canonical
    73	// Fizeau progress is surfaced directly from the ServiceEvent stream rather

    99			Data: progressPayload,

1	package agent
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"encoding/json"
     5		"testing"
     6		"time"
     7	
     8		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
     9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    11	)
    12	
    13	// TestDrainServiceEvents_CapturesFinalText covers the ddx-7bc0c8d5
    14	// normalizer stream: the upstream v0.8.0 final event carries a FinalText
    15	// field (agent-32e8ff5e) containing the harness's cleaned final response.
    16	// DDx must read it — NOT re-parse the raw stream per harness — so the
    17	// reviewer verdict extractor and other consumers see normalized text.
    18	//
    19	// Pre-migration DDx defined serviceFinalData without FinalText and the
    20	// field was silently dropped; result.Output was always empty and callers
    21	// fell back to per-harness parsers (extractOutputCodex, extractOutputClaude,
    22	// extractOutputPiGemini). Each of those had its own drift risk when
    23	// upstream stream shapes changed, which is exactly the maintenance burden
    24	// agent-32e8ff5e eliminates.
    25	func TestDrainServiceEvents_CapturesFinalText(t *testing.T) {
    26		events := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 2)
    27		finalPayload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
    28			"status":      "success",
    29			"exit_code":   0,
    30			"final_text":  "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nClean run.",
    31			"duration_ms": 1234,
    32		})
    33		require.NoError(t, err)
    34		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
    35			Type: "final",
    36			Time: time.Date(2026, 4, 20, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
    37			Data: finalPayload,
    38		}
    39		close(events)
    40	
    41		final, _, _ := drainServiceEvents(events)
    42		require.NotNil(t, final, "final event must drain even when only FinalText is populated")
    43		assert.Equal(t, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nClean run.", final.FinalText,
    44			"FinalText must round-trip verbatim — the reviewer verdict extractor depends on this being the harness's normalized output, not a raw stream frame")
    45	}
    46	
    47	// TestDrainServiceEvents_MissingFinalTextIsNotAnError covers the mixed
    48	// rollout period: not every harness/upstream version emits FinalText. An
    49	// absent FinalText must not crash the drain — it just yields empty Output
    50	// and callers handle empty gracefully (reviewer surfaces it as a parse
    51	// error, which feeds the retryable-failure path from ddx-f7ae036f).
    52	func TestDrainServiceEvents_MissingFinalTextIsNotAnError(t *testing.T) {
    53		events := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 2)
    54		finalPayload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
    55			"status":      "success",
    56			"exit_code":   0,
    57			"duration_ms": 500,
    58		})
    59		require.NoError(t, err)
    60		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
    61			Type: "final",
    62			Time: time.Date(2026, 4, 20, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
    63			Data: finalPayload,
    64		}
    65		close(events)
    66	
    67		final, _, _ := drainServiceEvents(events)
    68		require.NotNil(t, final)
    69		assert.Empty(t, final.FinalText,
    70			"missing FinalText must remain empty rather than synthesize placeholder — the reviewer-error path is the right response, not a fabricated string")
    71		assert.Equal(t, "success", final.Status)
    72	}

   139	func routingDecisionEventCandidates(in []RouteCandidate) []ServiceRoutingDecisionCandidate {
   140		if len(in) == 0 {
   141			return nil
   142		}
   143		out := make([]ServiceRoutingDecisionCandidate, len(in))
   144		for i, c := range in {
   145			out[i] = ServiceRoutingDecisionCandidate{
   146				Harness:            c.Harness,
   147				Provider:           c.Provider,
   148				Endpoint:           c.Endpoint,
   149				Model:              c.Model,
   150				Score:              c.Score,
   151				CostUSDPer1kTokens: c.CostUSDPer1kTokens,
   152				CostSource:         c.CostSource,
   153				Eligible:           c.Eligible,
   154				Reason:             c.Reason,
   155				FilterReason:       c.FilterReason,
   156				Components: ServiceRoutingDecisionComponents{
   157					Power:            c.Components.Power,
   158					Cost:             c.Components.Cost,
   159					CostClass:        c.Components.CostClass,
   160					LatencyMS:        c.Components.LatencyMS,
   161					SpeedTPS:         c.Components.SpeedTPS,
   162					SuccessRate:      c.Components.SuccessRate,
   163					QuotaOK:          c.Components.QuotaOK,
   164					QuotaPercentUsed: c.Components.QuotaPercentUsed,
   165					QuotaTrend:       c.Components.QuotaTrend,
   166					Capability:       c.Components.Capability,
   167				},
   168			}
   169		}
   170		return out
   171	}

   239	// ServiceFinalUsage is the public token-usage payload emitted on service
   240	// final events. Token-count fields are *int so callers can distinguish an
   241	// explicit upstream zero from "harness did not report this dimension":
   242	//
   243	//   - nil pointer  → the harness did not emit this token count (unknown).
   244	//     Consumers MUST NOT treat nil as zero or use it for budgeting.
   245	//   - non-nil *int → the harness emitted this exact value (including 0).
   246	//     Zero means the upstream provider explicitly reported zero usage; it
   247	//     is a real signal, not a gap.
   248	//
   249	// Consumers that aggregate or compare usage across runs should branch on
   250	// presence (nil vs non-nil) before reading the int. Per CONTRACT-003, the
   251	// service preserves provider provenance verbatim — emitters at the harness
   252	// boundary are forbidden from silently substituting zero for unknown.

   267	type ServiceFinalWarning struct {
   268		Code    string                       `json:"code"`
   269		Message string                       `json:"message,omitempty"`
   270		Sources []ServiceUsageSourceEvidence `json:"sources,omitempty"`
   271	}
   272	
   273	type ServiceUsageSourceEvidence struct {
   274		Source     string                   `json:"source"`
   275		Fresh      *bool                    `json:"fresh,omitempty"`
   276		CapturedAt string                   `json:"captured_at,omitempty"`
   277		Usage      *ServiceUsageTokenCounts `json:"usage,omitempty"`
   278		Warning    string                   `json:"warning,omitempty"`
   279	}
   280	
   281	type ServiceUsageTokenCounts struct {
   282		InputTokens      *int `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
   283		OutputTokens     *int `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
   284		CacheReadTokens  *int `json:"cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
   285		CacheWriteTokens *int `json:"cache_write_tokens,omitempty"`
   286		CacheTokens      *int `json:"cache_tokens,omitempty"`
   287		ReasoningTokens  *int `json:"reasoning_tokens,omitempty"`
   288		TotalTokens      *int `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
   289	}
   290	
   291	type ServiceRoutingActual struct {
   292		Harness            string   `json:"harness"`
   293		Provider           string   `json:"provider,omitempty"`
   294		Model              string   `json:"model"`
   295		FallbackChainFired []string `json:"fallback_chain_fired,omitempty"`
   296		FailureClass       string   `json:"failure_class,omitempty"`
   297		// Power is the catalog-projected power of the actually-dispatched
   298		// Model (CONTRACT-003 § Catalog Power Projection). 0 means
   299		// unknown/exact-pin-only/no catalog entry.
   300		Power int `json:"power,omitempty"`
   301	}
   302	
   303	// ServiceDecodedEvent is a typed view of one ServiceEvent. Exactly one payload
   304	// pointer is non-nil for a known event type.
   305	type ServiceDecodedEvent struct {
   306		Type     string
   307		Sequence int64
   308		Time     time.Time
   309		Metadata map[string]string
   310	
   311		TextDelta        *ServiceTextDeltaData
   312		ToolCall         *ServiceToolCallData
   313		ToolResult       *ServiceToolResultData
   314		Compaction       *ServiceCompactionData
   315		Progress         *ServiceProgressData
   316		RoutingDecision  *ServiceRoutingDecisionData
   317		Stall            *ServiceStallData
   318		Final            *ServiceFinalData
   319		Override         *ServiceOverrideData
   320		RejectedOverride *ServiceOverrideData
   321	}
   322	
   323	func DecodeServiceEvent(ev ServiceEvent) (ServiceDecodedEvent, error) {
   324		decoded := ServiceDecodedEvent{
   325			Type:     string(ev.Type),
   326			Sequence: ev.Sequence,
   327			Time:     ev.Time,
   328			Metadata: ev.Metadata,
   329		}
   330		switch string(ev.Type) {
   331		case ServiceEventTypeTextDelta:
   332			var payload ServiceTextDeltaData
   333			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   334				return decoded, err
   335			}
   336			decoded.TextDelta = &payload
   337		case ServiceEventTypeToolCall:
   338			var payload ServiceToolCallData
   339			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   340				return decoded, err
   341			}
   342			decoded.ToolCall = &payload
   343		case ServiceEventTypeToolResult:
   344			var payload ServiceToolResultData
   345			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   346				return decoded, err
   347			}
   348			decoded.ToolResult = &payload
   349		case ServiceEventTypeCompaction:
   350			var payload ServiceCompactionData
   351			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   352				return decoded, err
   353			}
   354			decoded.Compaction = &payload
   355		case ServiceEventTypeProgress:
   356			var payload ServiceProgressData
   357			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   358				return decoded, err
   359			}
   360			decoded.Progress = &payload
   361		case ServiceEventTypeRoutingDecision:
   362			var payload ServiceRoutingDecisionData
   363			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   364				return decoded, err
   365			}
   366			decoded.RoutingDecision = &payload
   367		case ServiceEventTypeStall:
   368			var payload ServiceStallData
   369			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   370				return decoded, err
   371			}
   372			decoded.Stall = &payload
   373		case ServiceEventTypeFinal:
   374			var payload ServiceFinalData
   375			if err := decodeServicePayload(ev, &payload); err != nil {
   376				return decoded, err
   377			}
   378			decoded.Final = &payload
   379		case ServiceEventTypeOverride:
   380			var payload ServiceOverrideData

1	package agent
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"encoding/json"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"strconv"
     7		"strings"
     8		"time"
     9	
    10		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
    11	)
    12	
    13	// Session log JSONL schema reference.
    14	//
    15	// The agent harness writes one event per line to .ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl
    16	// (or to the per-run SessionLogDir override). Every line has the envelope:
    17	//
    18	//	{"session_id": string, "seq": int, "type": string, "ts": RFC3339Nano, "data": {...}}
    19	//
    20	// The standard schema is always emitted and is what existing tooling consumes.
    21	// Diagnostic logging is opt-in via DDX_LOG_VERBOSE=1; when set, the writer adds
    22	// raw body fields so a session can be replayed and root-caused without loss of
    23	// inputs and outputs. Diagnostic fields are strictly additive: event ordering
    24	// and event types stay the same across harnesses.
    25	//
    26	// Per-event-type fields:
    27	//
    28	//	session.start:
    29	//	  data.model       string  — model id reported by the harness
    30	//	  data.session_id  string  — session id from the harness CLI
    31	//	  data.bead_id     string  — bead under which the session ran (may be "")
    32	//	  data.elapsed_ms  int     — ms since session start (always 0 here)
    33	//	  data.harness     string  — "claude" | "fiz" | etc.
    34	//
    35	//	llm.request:
    36	//	  data.attempt_index int        — request attempt counter (0-based)
    37	//	  data.messages      []object   — conversation messages (last user message hint used by the renderer)
    38	//
    39	//	llm.response:
    40	//	  data.model         string  — resolved model id
    41	//	  data.latency_ms    int     — per-call wall time (request → response)
    42	//	  data.tool_calls    []{name} — tool_use names emitted in this turn
    43	//	  data.turn          int     — 1-based assistant turn count
    44	//	  data.bead_id       string
    45	//	  data.elapsed_ms    int     — ms since session start
    46	//	  data.input_tokens  int     — running input tokens (latest seen)
    47	//	  data.output_tokens int     — running output tokens (latest seen)
    48	//	  data.attempt.cost.raw.total_tokens int — running total
    49	//
    50	//	llm.response (diagnostic adds):
    51	//	  data.content       string  — concatenated assistant text blocks for this turn
    52	//	  data.finish_reason string  — claude stop_reason ("end_turn", "tool_use", "max_tokens", ...)
    53	//	  data.usage object:
    54	//	    input_tokens                int
    55	//	    output_tokens               int
    56	//	    cache_creation_input_tokens int
    57	//	    cache_read_input_tokens     int
    58	//	    total_tokens                int
    59	//
    60	//	tool.call:
    61	//	  data.tool       string         — tool name
    62	//	  data.input      object         — tool arguments as decoded JSON
    63	//	  data.bead_id    string
    64	//	  data.elapsed_ms int            — ms since session start
    65	//	  data.turn       int            — assistant turn that issued the call
    66	//
    67	//
    68	//	tool.result:
    69	//	  data.tool         string
    70	//	  data.output_bytes int
    71	//	  data.output_excerpt string — compact first-line excerpt safe for progress logs
    72	//	  data.duration_ms  int     — wall time from tool_use → tool_result
    73	//	  data.error        string  — non-empty when tool_result is_error=true; empty otherwise
    74	//	  data.bead_id      string
    75	//	  data.elapsed_ms   int
    76	//	  data.turn         int
    77	//
    78	//	tool.result (diagnostic adds):
    79	//	  data.output string — tool_result content (joined if structured)
    80	//
    81	// Diagnostic logging is off by default; existing consumers of the standard
    82	// schema (TailSessionLogs, FormatSessionLogLines, the server worker log
    83	// endpoint) keep working unchanged because they tolerate extra fields.
    84	
    85	// FormatSessionLogLines formats Fizeau-style JSONL log entries into readable progress.
    86	// It is used by both the CLI (local execute-loop) and the server worker log endpoint.
    87	func FormatSessionLogLines(lines []string) string {
    88		var sb strings.Builder
    89		for _, line := range lines {
    90			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
    91			if line == "" {
    92				continue
    93			}
    94			var entry map[string]any
    95			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry); err != nil {
    96				continue
    97			}
    98			entryType, _ := entry["type"].(string)
    99			switch entryType {
   100			case "session.start":
   101				model, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any)["model"].(string)
   102				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "▶ session started (model: %s)\n", model)
   103			case "llm.request":
   104				data, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
   105				attemptIdx, _ := data["attempt_index"].(float64)
   106				requestBytes := encodedPayloadSize(data["messages"])
   107				// Extract a hint from the last user message in the conversation.
   108				promptHint := ""
   109				if msgs, ok := data["messages"].([]any); ok {
   110					for i := len(msgs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
   111						if msg, ok := msgs[i].(map[string]any); ok {
   112							if role, _ := msg["role"].(string); role == "user" {
   113								if content, _ := msg["content"].(string); content != "" {
   114									promptHint = " [" + truncateStr(strings.TrimSpace(content), 60) + "]"
   115								}
   116								break
   117							}
   118						}
   119					}
   120				}
   121				sizeHint := formatSizeSuffix("req", requestBytes)
   122				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  → llm request (attempt %.0f%s)%s\n", attemptIdx, sizeHint, promptHint)
   123			case "llm.response":
   124				data, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
   125				model, _ := data["model"].(string)
   126				latency, _ := data["latency_ms"].(float64)
   127				responseBytes := progressInt(data, "response_bytes")
   128				if responseBytes <= 0 {
   129					responseBytes = encodedPayloadSize(data["content"])
   130				}
   131				thinkingBytes := progressInt(data, "thinking_bytes")
   132				toolInputBytes := progressInt(data, "tool_input_bytes")
   133				// Tokens: data.attempt.cost.raw.total_tokens
   134				var tokens float64
   135				if attempt, ok := data["attempt"].(map[string]any); ok {
   136					if cost, ok := attempt["cost"].(map[string]any); ok {
   137						if raw, ok := cost["raw"].(map[string]any); ok {
   138							tokens, _ = raw["total_tokens"].(float64)
   139						}
   140					}
   141				}
   142				// Tool calls from response
   143				toolCalls, _ := data["tool_calls"].([]any)
   144				finishReason, _ := data["finish_reason"].(string)
   145				suffix := ""
   146				if len(toolCalls) > 0 {
   147					var names []string
   148					for _, tc := range toolCalls {
   149						if tcm, ok := tc.(map[string]any); ok {
   150							n, _ := tcm["name"].(string)
   151							if n != "" {
   152								names = append(names, n)
   153							}
   154						}
   155					}
   156					suffix = fmt.Sprintf(" → %s", strings.Join(names, ", "))
   157				} else if finishReason != "" {
   158					suffix = fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", finishReason)
   159				}
   160				byteHints := formatPayloadHints([]payloadHint{
   161					{label: "text", bytes: responseBytes},
   162					{label: "think", bytes: thinkingBytes},
   163					{label: "tool_in", bytes: toolInputBytes},
   164				})
   165				if byteHints != "" {
   166					byteHints = ", " + byteHints
   167				}
   168				throughput := formatTokenThroughput(int(tokens), int(latency))
   169				if throughput != "" {
   170					throughput = ", " + throughput
   171				}
   172				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  ← llm response (%.0f tokens, %.1fs%s%s) %s%s\n", tokens, latency/1000, throughput, byteHints, model, suffix)
   173			case "llm.delta":
   174				// Skip deltas — too verbose for summary
   175			case "progress":
   176				if line := formatProgressLogEntry(entry); line != "" {
   177					fmt.Fprint(&sb, line)
   178				}
   179			case "tool.call":
   180				data, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
   181				name, _ := data["tool"].(string)
   182				inp, _ := data["input"].(map[string]any)
   183				dur, _ := data["duration_ms"].(float64)
   184				inputBytes := progressInt(data, "input_bytes")
   185				if inputBytes <= 0 {
   186					inputBytes = encodedPayloadSize(inp)
   187				}
   188				output, _ := data["output"].(string)
   189				outputBytes := progressInt(data, "output_bytes")
   190				if outputBytes <= 0 {
   191					outputBytes = len([]byte(output))
   192				}
   193				argHint := ""
   194				if len(inp) > 0 {
   195					// Prefer path/command/query keys for display
   196					for _, key := range []string{"path", "command", "query", "file"} {
   197						if v, ok := inp[key]; ok {
   198							argHint = fmt.Sprintf("%v", v)
   199							break
   200						}
   201					}
   202					if argHint == "" {
   203						for _, v := range inp {
   204							argHint = fmt.Sprintf("%v", v)
   205							break
   206						}
   207					}
   208					argHint = compactToolDisplay("", argHint)
   209				}
   210				errMsg, _ := data["error"].(string)
   211				errSuffix := ""
   212				if errMsg != "" {
   213					errSuffix = fmt.Sprintf(" ❌ %s", truncateStr(errMsg, 40))
   214				}
   215				durSuffix := ""
   216				if dur > 0 {
   217					durSuffix = fmt.Sprintf(" (%.1fs)", dur/1000)
   218				}
   219				sizeSuffix := formatPayloadHints([]payloadHint{
   220					{label: "in", bytes: inputBytes},

1	package agent
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"io"
     6		"path/filepath"
     7		"time"
     8	)
     9	
    10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
    11	type Config struct {
    12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
    13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
    14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
    15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
    16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
    17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
    18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
    19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
    20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
    21	}
    22	
    23	// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
    24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
    25	type RouteFlags struct {
    26		Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
    27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
    28		Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
    29		ModelRef    string // --model-ref: catalog model reference
    30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
    31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
    32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
    33	}
    34	
    35	// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
    36	type RunArgs struct {
    37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
    38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
    39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
    40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
    41		Context       context.Context
    42		Harness       string
    43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
    44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
    45		PromptSource  string
    46		Correlation   map[string]string
    47		Model         string
    48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
    49		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved via the model catalog
    50		Effort        string
    51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
    52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
    53		WorkDir       string
    54		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
    55		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
    56		Role          string
    57		CorrelationID string
    58	}
    59	
    60	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
    61	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
    62	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
    63	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
    64	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
    65	// runtime intent remain.
    66	//
    67	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
    68	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
    69		Prompt                string
    70		PromptFile            string
    71		PromptSource          string
    72		Output                io.Writer
    73		Correlation           map[string]string
    74		Record                bool
    75		Replay                bool
    76		WorkDir               string
    77		EstimatedPromptTokens int
    78		RequiresTools         bool
    79		SessionLogDirOverride string
    80		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, and PermissionsOverride let a caller
    81		// pin one of the durable knobs from rcfg for this single invocation
    82		// without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig. SD-024 step B22d-d:
    83		// execute-bead pins permissions=unrestricted; the post-merge reviewer
    84		// pins harness/model to the reviewer-tier selection.
    85		HarnessOverride     string
    86		ModelOverride       string
    87		PermissionsOverride string
    88		Role                string
    89		CorrelationID       string
    90		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
    91		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
    92		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
    93		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).
    94		MinPowerOverride int
    95	}
    96	
    97	// Result holds the output of an agent invocation.
    98	type Result struct {
    99		Harness                     string  `json:"harness"`
   100		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
   101		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
   102		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
   103		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
   104		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
   105		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64 `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
   106		PredictedCostSource         string  `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
   107		ExitCode                    int     `json:"exit_code"`
   108		Output                      string  `json:"output"`
   109		CondensedOutput             string  `json:"condensed_output,omitempty"`
   110		Stderr                      string  `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
   111		// Routing evidence populated by the embedded agent harness (RunAgent) or
   112		// the script harness (RunScript). Used by ExecuteBead to record kind:routing
   113		// evidence on the bead.
   114		RouteReason     string          `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
   115		ResolvedBaseURL string          `json:"resolved_base_url,omitempty"`
   116		Tokens          int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
   117		InputTokens     int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
   118		CachedTokens    int             `json:"cached_tokens,omitempty"`
   119		OutputTokens    int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
   120		CostUSD         float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
   121		DurationMS      int             `json:"duration_ms"`
   122		Error           string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
   123		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`       // populated by agent, nil for subprocess
   124		AgentSessionID  string          `json:"agent_session_id,omitempty"` // agent session ID for event log cross-reference
   125	}
   126	
   127	// SessionEntry is written to the session log.
   128	type SessionEntry struct {
   129		ID              string            `json:"id"`
   130		Timestamp       time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
   131		Harness         string            `json:"harness"`
   132		Provider        string            `json:"provider,omitempty"`
   133		Surface         string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`
   134		CanonicalTarget string            `json:"canonical_target,omitempty"`
   135		BaseURL         string            `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
   136		BillingMode     string            `json:"billingMode,omitempty"`
   137		Model           string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
   138		PromptLen       int               `json:"prompt_len"`
   139		Prompt          string            `json:"prompt,omitempty"`
   140		PromptSource    string            `json:"prompt_source,omitempty"`
   141		Response        string            `json:"response,omitempty"`
   142		Correlation     map[string]string `json:"correlation,omitempty"`
   143		NativeSessionID string            `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
   144		NativeLogRef    string            `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`
   145		TraceID         string            `json:"trace_id,omitempty"`
   146		SpanID          string            `json:"span_id,omitempty"`
   147		Stderr          string            `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
   148		Tokens          int               `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
   149		InputTokens     int               `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
   150		CachedTokens    int               `json:"cached_tokens,omitempty"`
   151		OutputTokens    int               `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
   152		CostUSD         float64           `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
   153		Duration        int               `json:"duration_ms"`
   154		ExitCode        int               `json:"exit_code"`
   155		Error           string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
   156		TotalTokens     int               `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"` // input + output; populated on every run
   157		BaseRev         string            `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`     // git SHA the execution started from (execute-bead only)
   158		ResultRev       string            `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`   // git SHA of landed/preserved iteration (execute-bead only)
   159		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry   `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`   // normalized tool-call stream captured at drain (Story 16)
   160	}
   161	
   162	// ProviderStatus tracks provider connectivity and credit status.
   163	type ProviderStatus struct {
   164		Reachable bool   `json:"reachable"`
   165		CreditsOK bool   `json:"credits_ok,omitempty"` // false if out of credits/quota
   166		Error     string `json:"error,omitempty"`
   167	}
   168	
   169	// HarnessCapabilities describes the effective capabilities for a harness.
   170	type HarnessCapabilities struct {
   171		Harness             string            `json:"harness"`
   172		Available           bool              `json:"available"`
   173		Binary              string            `json:"binary"`
   174		Path                string            `json:"path,omitempty"`
   175		Model               string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
   176		Models              []string          `json:"models,omitempty"`
   177		ReasoningLevels     []string          `json:"reasoning_levels,omitempty"`
   178		Surface             string            `json:"surface,omitempty"`          // catalog surface identifier
   179		CostClass           string            `json:"cost_class,omitempty"`       // local, cheap, medium, expensive
   180		IsLocal             bool              `json:"is_local"`                   // true if embedded/local (no cloud cost)
   181		ExactPinSupport     bool              `json:"exact_pin_support"`          // true if harness accepts exact model pin
   182		ProfileMappings     map[string]string `json:"profile_mappings,omitempty"` // effective profile → model for this harness
   183		SupportsEffort      bool              `json:"supports_effort"`            // true if harness has effort/reasoning flag
   184		SupportsPermissions bool              `json:"supports_permissions"`       // true if harness has permission-level flags
   185	}
   186	
   187	// ToolCallEntry records one tool execution during an agent run.
   188	// Mirrors the agent library's ToolCallLog without importing it in types.
   189	type ToolCallEntry struct {
   190		Tool     string `json:"tool"`
   191		Input    string `json:"input"`
   192		Output   string `json:"output,omitempty"`
   193		Duration int    `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
   194		Error    string `json:"error,omitempty"`
   195	}
   196	
   197	// ComparisonArm holds the result of one harness arm in a comparison.
   198	type ComparisonArm struct {
   199		Harness      string          `json:"harness"`
   200		Model        string          `json:"model,omitempty"`
   201		Output       string          `json:"output"`
   202		Diff         string          `json:"diff,omitempty"`         // git diff of side effects
   203		ToolCalls    []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`   // agent tool call log (nil for subprocess)
   204		PostRunOut   string          `json:"post_run_out,omitempty"` // post-run command output
   205		PostRunOK    *bool           `json:"post_run_ok,omitempty"`  // post-run pass/fail
   206		Tokens       int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
   207		InputTokens  int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
   208		OutputTokens int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
   209		CostUSD      float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
   210		DurationMS   int             `json:"duration_ms"`
   211		ExitCode     int             `json:"exit_code"`
   212		Error        string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
   213	}
   214	
   215	// ComparisonGrade holds the evaluation of one arm by a grading harness.
   216	type ComparisonGrade struct {
   217		Arm       string `json:"arm"`
   218		Score     int    `json:"score"`
   219		MaxScore  int    `json:"max_score"`
   220		Pass      bool   `json:"pass"`
   221		Rationale string `json:"rationale"`
   222	}
   223	
   224	// ComparisonRecord is the complete record of a comparison run.
   225	type ComparisonRecord struct {
   226		ID        string            `json:"id"`
   227		Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
   228		Prompt    string            `json:"prompt"`
   229		Arms      []ComparisonArm   `json:"arms"`
   230		Grades    []ComparisonGrade `json:"grades,omitempty"`
   231	}
   232	
   233	// CandidateState captures contract-derived routing state for a candidate.
   234	type CandidateState struct {
   235		Installed     bool                   `json:"installed"`
   236		Reachable     bool                   `json:"reachable"`
   237		Authenticated bool                   `json:"authenticated"`
   238		QuotaOK       bool                   `json:"quota_ok"`
   239		QuotaState    string                 `json:"quota_state,omitempty"` // ok, blocked, unknown
   240		Degraded      bool                   `json:"degraded"`
